description: AR300M / AR300M-Ext (NOR boot). NOT recommended for general use — NAND is 128MB vs NOR's 16MB. NOR is useful as automatic failsafe recovery partition. NOR boot via switch RIGHT (bootm 0x9f050000) or bootcount method (fw_setenv bootcount 3). Switch-based NOR boot may not work on all U-Boot versions.

switch.description: Physical slide switch. Designed to select NAND vs NOR boot when boot_dev=on. Switch LEFT → NAND (nboot), switch RIGHT → NOR (bootm 0x9f050000). NOR boot via switch may not work on all U-Boot versions. This NOR model is NOT recommended for general use — NAND is 128MB vs NOR's 16MB.
switch.recommendation: Use glinet-gl-ar300m-nand (switch LEFT) instead. NOR is only useful as automatic failsafe fallback.

right.label: Away from USB port
right.notes: Switch RIGHT triggers NOR boot via bootm 0x9f050000 (GL_BOOT_ADDR). May not work on all U-Boot versions. Use bootcount method (fw_setenv bootcount 3) as reliable fallback. Only 16MB — very limited package space. Source: U-Boot device_check.c:71-113.

nor.notes: Partition layout when booted from NOR. MTD indices differ from NAND boot. firmware=mtd2 contains combined kernel+rootfs. rootfs_data=mtd5 is jffs2 overlay (~8.9MB available).

recovery-http.description: U-Boot HTTP recovery via pepe2k/u-boot_mod. Same U-Boot as NAND model. Switch position does not affect recovery mode.
recovery-http.reset_instructions: Hold reset button while connecting power. Hold for ~5 seconds until LEDs flash. Switch position does not matter for U-Boot recovery mode.
recovery-http.led_pattern: LEDs flash 5 times during U-Boot entry

nor_boot_bootcount.notes: NOR boot via bootcount method (fw_setenv bootcount 3). Previously worked. CAVEAT: With working NAND kernel, nboot in select_boot_dev() boots NAND first and never returns to NOR bootm. Reliable NOR boot via bootcount may require NAND kernel to be broken. Verified when it did work: glinet,gl-ar300m-nor DTB, squashfs rootfs on NOR mtd4, jffs2 overlay 8.9MB. Hostname: ar300-nor-recovery.

nor_boot_switch.notes: Switch RIGHT is designed to boot NOR via bootm 0x9f050000 (GL_BOOT_ADDR). Tested 2026-05-17: NOR kernel starts but PANICS. ROOT CAUSE CONFIRMED: D-cache not flushed after LZMA decompression (cmd_bootm.c bug). MIPS 24Kc has zero hardware I/D coherency. Uncached alias 0xbf050000 also fails — destination writes still dirty. Fix applied in fork https://github.com/Amperstrand/uboot-for-qca95xx (branch conwrt-fix). NOT YET TESTED ON DEVICE. See recipes/glinet/ar300m/README.md for full analysis.

nand_boot_after_nor.notes: SUCCESS. After NOR boot (bootcount=4), switch LEFT, power cycle → NAND booted correctly (hostname: switchleft, glinet,gl-ar300m-nand). Bootcount auto-reset to 0.

sysupgrade.description: AR300M uses glinet_nand_nor_do_upgrade() which auto-detects image type and flashes the correct partition regardless of current boot source.
sysupgrade.mechanism: Auto-detects by magic number: 0x27051956 (U-Boot Image Magic) → NOR upgrade (writes to NOR firmware partition), tar archive → NAND upgrade (writes to NAND kernel + UBI partitions).
sysupgrade.nor_firmware_format: Raw uImage (U-Boot Image Magic 0x27051956). Same as NAND model's NOR image.
sysupgrade.notes: When booted from NOR, you can flash NAND firmware via sysupgrade. The tar is detected and written to NAND partitions. Next boot will be NAND (bootcount=0). This is the intended recovery path: boot NOR → sysupgrade NAND → reboot to NAND.
